tasks.direction_relation_note: This is the first executable slice for the new roadmap because it turns the context-first direction into an ordered, reviewable audit before deeper refactors or provider-boundary work starts.
tasks.title: Freeze hotspot split order and vocabulary gaps
tasks.description: Turn the architecture debt map and the active roadmap into one actionable audit: confirm the split order for shell presentation, conversation runtime, planning controller, and parallel mode service, and record the vocabulary mismatches still visible across docs and operator-facing copy.
tasks.priority_reason: The roadmap is only useful if the first follow-up converts it into concrete hotspot order and vocabulary cleanup targets without touching shipped truth prematurely.

tasks.direction_relation_note: The new roadmap requires an explicit Codex-only assumption audit before external terminal agents can be discussed as clean product boundaries.
tasks.title: Map Codex-only assumptions into capability targets
tasks.description: Audit where `codex app-server`, Codex-specific session semantics, and Codex-flavored approval or resume assumptions are embedded today, then restate those spots as target capability notes around `InteractiveTurnRuntime`, `StartupProbe`, `SessionCatalog`, and `TerminalBridgeAttachment`.
tasks.priority_reason: External-agent research should not drift into vague abstraction until the current Codex coupling points are named precisely.

tasks.direction_relation_note: Shell presentation is the first hotspot named in the roadmap and should become the first concrete refactor slice after the audit firms up the split order.
tasks.title: Prepare the first shell-presentation boundary slice
tasks.description: Use the audit output to define a small refactor slice that separates shell wording and projection responsibilities from layout or rendering mechanics in the shell presentation cluster.
tasks.priority_reason: This is a likely next implementation slice, but the exact cut should stay proposed until the hotspot audit locks the order and surface area.

tasks.direction_relation_note: The research direction starts with a comparison matrix only after the Codex-only boundary audit makes the required capabilities explicit.
tasks.title: Compare local attach, wrapper, tunnel, and proxy bridge paths
tasks.description: Write the research matrix that compares pre-opened local terminal attach, controlled local wrappers, SSH or tunnel approaches, and proxy or vibeproxy-style mediation across UX, streaming fidelity, interrupt handling, approval handling, recovery, security, portability, and architecture fit.
tasks.priority_reason: Research should begin only after the capability vocabulary is explicit enough to compare paths on product terms instead of transport trivia.

tasks.direction_relation_note: The roadmap requires one primary bridge path and one fallback before any provider-specific spike can be treated as intentional product work.
tasks.title: Choose the primary and fallback terminal bridge paths
tasks.description: Turn the completed research matrix into one decision note that names the primary bridge path, the fallback path, and the reasons for deferring the remaining transport options for now.
tasks.priority_reason: The decision note is a real deliverable for the roadmap, but it should stay proposed until the comparison matrix exists.
